Class TaskService

java.lang.Object
de.hallerweb.enterprise.prioritize.service.project.TaskService
@Service @Transactional public class TaskService extends Object
Manages tasks on a project's Blackboard. Authorization is delegated to ProjectService: any manager or member of the owning project may work on its tasks.
Author:
peter haller

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static final record 
    TaskService.TaskData
    Editable task fields, decoupling the service from HTTP DTOs.
    static final record 
    TaskService.TrackingSummary
    Aggregated time-tracking total for a task.
    static final record 
    TaskService.WorkSession
    One tracked work session on a task: a single start-to-stop interval.

    TaskService.TrackingSummary
    getTrackingSummary(Long taskId, PUser user)
    Returns the total time tracked on a task: the sum of all completed spans plus, if tracking is currently running, the open span counted live up to now.
    List<TaskService.WorkSession>
    getWorkSessions(Long taskId, PUser user)
    Returns the individual work sessions tracked on a task: each completed span, plus the open session (with until = null, counted live up to now) if tracking is running.
    Task
    startTracking(Long taskId, PUser user)
    Starts time tracking on a task: opens a new TimeSpan.TimeSpanType.TIME_TRACKER span with the current server time and promotes the task to TaskStatus.STARTED.
    Task
    stopTracking(Long taskId, PUser user)
    Stops time tracking on a task: closes the running span with the current server time, moves it into the task's
    invalid reference 
    timeSpent
    history and sets the task to TaskStatus.STOPPED.
    Task
    toggleTracking(Long taskId, PUser user)
    Toggles time tracking on a task: stops it if running, otherwise starts it.

  • Constructor Details

    • TaskService

      public TaskService()

  • Method Details

    • createTask

      public Task createTask(Long projectId, TaskService.TaskData data, PUser user)
      Creates a task on the given project's blackboard with status TaskStatus.CREATED.
      Parameters:
      projectId - the owning project's id
      data - the task's initial field values
      user - the requesting user (must be manager or member)
      Returns:
      the persisted task

    • createScheduledTask

      public Task createScheduledTask(Project project, String name, String description, int priority)
      Creates a task from a recurring schedule's template on project's blackboard with status TaskStatus.CREATED. This is a trusted system path invoked by the scheduler, not a user: it performs no membership check, mirroring the user-less ingest paths (MQTT / telemetry). It stays decoupled from the scheduling model — the caller unpacks the template.
      Parameters:
      project - the owning project (its blackboard receives the task)
      name - the generated task's name
      description - the generated task's description
      priority - the generated task's priority
      Returns:
      the persisted task

    • getTask

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public Task getTask(Long taskId, PUser user)

    • getTasksForProject

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public List<Task> getTasksForProject(Long projectId, PUser user)

    • updateTask

      public Task updateTask(Long taskId, TaskService.TaskData data, PUser user)

    • deleteTask

      public void deleteTask(Long taskId, PUser user)

    • assignTask

      public Task assignTask(Long taskId, Long actorId, PUser user)
      Assigns a task to an actor (person or machine). If the task is still in an early status it is promoted to TaskStatus.ASSIGNED.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the task id
      actorId - the id of the actor to assign
      user - the requesting user
      Returns:
      the updated task

    • unassignTask

      public Task unassignTask(Long taskId, PUser user)

    • assignGoal

      public Task assignGoal(Long taskId, Long goalId, PUser user)
      Assigns the task to one of its own project's goals, so it contributes to that goal's progress. The goal must belong to the task's project.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the task id
      goalId - the id of a goal in the task's project
      user - the requesting user (must be manager or member)
      Returns:
      the updated task
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if the goal is not part of the task's project

    • unassignGoal

      public Task unassignGoal(Long taskId, PUser user)

    • changeStatus

      public Task changeStatus(Long taskId, TaskStatus newStatus, PUser user)
      Changes a task's status. Transitions out of a terminal status (TaskStatus.CLOSED/TaskStatus.CANCELLED) are rejected.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the task id
      newStatus - the target status
      user - the requesting user
      Returns:
      the updated task
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the task is already in a terminal status

    • startTracking

      public Task startTracking(Long taskId, PUser user)
      Starts time tracking on a task: opens a new TimeSpan.TimeSpanType.TIME_TRACKER span with the current server time and promotes the task to TaskStatus.STARTED.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the task id
      user - the requesting user (must be manager or member); recorded on the span
      Returns:
      the updated task
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if tracking is already running for this task

    • stopTracking

      public Task stopTracking(Long taskId, PUser user)
      Stops time tracking on a task: closes the running span with the current server time, moves it into the task's
      invalid reference 
      timeSpent
      history and sets the task to TaskStatus.STOPPED.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the task id
      user - the requesting user (must be manager or member)
      Returns:
      the updated task
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if no tracking is currently running for this task

    • toggleTracking

      public Task toggleTracking(Long taskId, PUser user)
      Toggles time tracking on a task: stops it if running, otherwise starts it. This is the entry point an NFC scan of a NfcUnit.NfcUnitType.TIMETRACKER tag maps to.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the task id
      user - the requesting user (must be manager or member)
      Returns:
      the updated task

    • getTrackingSummary

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public TaskService.TrackingSummary getTrackingSummary(Long taskId, PUser user)
      Returns the total time tracked on a task: the sum of all completed spans plus, if tracking is currently running, the open span counted live up to now. Manager or member.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the task id
      user - the requesting user
      Returns:
      the aggregated tracking total

    • getWorkSessions

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public List<TaskService.WorkSession> getWorkSessions(Long taskId, PUser user)
      Returns the individual work sessions tracked on a task: each completed span, plus the open session (with until = null, counted live up to now) if tracking is running. Completed sessions come first, the running one last. The aggregate total is getTrackingSummary(Long, PUser). Manager or member.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the task id
      user - the requesting user
      Returns:
      the tracked work sessions, empty if nothing has been tracked yet
